name: DREsS CASE Uses Corruption Rather Than LLM Prompting for Synthetic Essays
statement: Yoo et al. use the DREsS_CASE corruption method to generate 40,185 synthetic lower-quality essay samples for rubric-based EFL automated essay scoring training augmentation.
time_horizon: ACL 2025 paper already published in the Lit Review Studio baseline and reviewed on 2026-06-02.
caveats: This is not an LLM generation method.
The generated samples are training augmentation data, not validation/test data.
evaluation_method: Automated essay scoring evaluation with real validation/test data and weighted-kappa-style agreement metrics.
limitations: The method is bounded to EFL essay scoring.
CASE corruption does not represent profile-conditioned or prompt-conditioned LLM generation.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The staged finding reuses the already-published source boundary for DREsS_CASE synthetic samples.
